user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
however, i would recommend two things, very seriously!

1) what i wrote in the welcome email: don't use AI to help you understand any explainations.
AIs are good when you have a long text, paper etc. or tons of emails, and you don't have time to read them all, you can sum them with that.
I know how they work, they won't help you. Take the effort to think.
I don't want to see any AI generated explaination in this chat. You can post, no ban for that, but its your interest to not fool yourself.

2) i had a prof who had a very deep impact on me by using expressions when he explained things like "i think that..", "in my opinion..", "as much as I see.."
I loved it. And it taught me something: 
recognize if you aren't sure in a topic. Emphatize that it is your personal understanding about the topic, instead of telling it like it is the truth.
Very important. 

If you are unsure about some info, logical connection, etc. you better ask me or do a research together, and it will become a very interesting and exciting common journey that way :))
instead of telling false infos to each other. 

It's not a shame if you don't understand something.

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
ZERO GAMMA

if you look at any GEX charts I provide, or at OD heatmaps, you will see multiple zero gamma lines.
Every value that can go from positive to negative and vice versa can be zero too. Charm, vanna, vomma, rho, parmicharm, anything...

The misconception arises from that in naive GEX models the assumption is used that customers sell calls and buy puts. 
So they handle the OI that way, and they will get a gamma profile like this one:
comment.raw_body:

comment.body:
now, the concept of zero gamma is that thats where market makers shift from bullish to bearish and bearish to bullish.... this is what you heard, right?
bcs negative gamma makes them hedge into the direction, and positive gamma makes them to hedge against it
